comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "Line 715: `review_decision=pr_review_decision` — passed to `compute_status_indicators()`" (doc line 66)
Reality: The actual line number in `real.py` is ~735, not 715. The line number has drifted.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/real.py`

Suggested fix: Replace the three specific line numbers (573, 599, 715) in the Wiring section with a name-based source pointer — line numbers go stale silently with every edit.

comments.body:
Fixed: Replaced all specific line numbers with name-based source pointers to prevent drift.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — documents specific line numbers that will change.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py`

Risk: The "Display Logic" section cites lines 67-108 for `compute_status_indicators()`. The "GraphQL Source" section cites lines 76, 149, 198 for `graphql_queries.py`. The "Wiring in real.py" section cites lines 573, 599, 715. Any edit to these files silently invalidates these references. Consider replacing all line-number references with name-based source pointers (e.g., `See `compute_status_indicators()` in `lifecycle.py``).

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed: Replaced all line number references (573, 599, 715 in Wiring; 67-108 in Display Logic; 76, 149, 198 in GraphQL; 201 in types.py) with name-based source pointers.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 00:53 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "`None` means the PR has no review state (e.g., no reviewers assigned, or `REVIEW_REQUIRED` maps to no indicator)."
Reality: The parenthetical conflates two separate cases. `None` is the GraphQL null case (no reviewers assigned or review not configured). `REVIEW_REQUIRED` is a distinct string value that produces no display indicator — it does not 'map to' `None`. The phrasing implies `REVIEW_REQUIRED` results in a null field value, which is incorrect.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py` (see `_build_indicators()`) and `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/types.py:201`

Suggested fix: Split the explanation — e.g., "`None` means the PR has no review state (e.g., no reviewers assigned). Both `None` and `REVIEW_REQUIRED` produce no display indicator."

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed in cfae8af7c: Clarified that None and REVIEW_REQUIRED are distinct cases — None means no review state (no reviewers assigned), while REVIEW_REQUIRED means reviewers are assigned but haven't completed review. Both produce no indicator but for different reasons.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 01:07 UTC_
